metadata.title: Government Schemes [India & States] Current Affairs MCQs

questions.question: Which state government launched Mukhyamantri Gramotthan Yojana to provide urban-level facilities in rural areas?
questions.explanation: Gujarat Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel launched the state-wide Mukhyamantri Gramotthan Yojana from Bhadran in Anand district. The scheme aims to provide urban-level facilities in rural areas and reduce the rural–urban development gap. In the first phase, 114 villages that are Taluka headquarters will be upgraded with modern infrastructure. The Chief Minister laid the foundation stone for 2,666 Gram Panchayat Ghar-cum-Talati Awas buildings worth ₹663 crore. The Gramotthan Abhiyan will later cover all villages with a population above 10,000. The initiative focuses on inclusive and balanced rural development across Gujarat.

questions.question: Gyan Bharatam mission is a flagship initiative of which ministry?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

questions.explanation: Gyan Bharatam is a flagship Ministry of Culture initiative announced in Union Budget 2025–26 to preserve India’s manuscript heritage. It aligns with the vision of Viksit Bharat 2047 by linking cultural preservation with human capital development. The Standing Finance Committee approved ₹491.66 crore for 2025–2031. The programme works on survey, conservation, digitisation, translation, research, and outreach. More than 7.5 lakh manuscripts have been digitised, with 1.29 lakh available on the Gyan Bharatam portal.

questions.question: Pradhan Mantri Dakshta Aur Kushalta Sampann Hitgrahi Yojana (PM-DAKSH) is implemented by which Ministry?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

answer.label: C
answer.text: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

questions.explanation: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment implements Pradhan Mantri Dakshta Aur Kushalta Sampann Hitgrahi Yojana (PM-DAKSH) as a Central Sector Scheme. It provides skill training to Scheduled Castes, Other Backward Classes, Economically Weaker Sections, De-notified Tribes and Safai Karamcharis. Training is conducted in National Skill Qualification Framework (NSQF) compliant courses aligned with local market demand. Training institutes must ensure at least 70 percent wage or self-employment outcomes.

questions.question: Ayushman Sahakar Scheme is implemented by which organization?

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: National Cooperative Development Corporation (NCDC), a statutory body under Ministry of Cooperation, implements Ayushman Sahakar Scheme to promote cooperative healthcare. The scheme aligns with National Health Policy 2017 to provide affordable and holistic healthcare through cooperatives. Financial assistance is given as term loan or investment loan after technical and financial appraisal. The scheme supports hospitals, AYUSH, medical colleges, telemedicine, digital health and insurance services. Loan period is up to 8 years with moratorium, and women-majority cooperatives get 1% interest rebate. The initiative promotes community participation and sustainable cooperative healthcare delivery.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the Banglar Yuva Sathi Scheme 2026 to support unemployed youth?
questions.explanation: The Government of West Bengal has launched the Banglar Yuva Sathi Scheme 2026 to support unemployed youth in the state. The scheme was announced by Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee during the state’s interim budget presentation. Under this initiative, eligible unemployed youth will receive a monthly allowance of ₹1,500 as transitional financial support. The scheme aims to assist educated but unemployed individuals by providing a financial support while they search for employment, rather than serving as a permanent income source. Although initially planned for launch in August 2026, the programme has been fast-tracked and will officially begin on April 1, 2026.

questions.question: Which state government has introduced the ‘Mahila Khushhali Yojna’ for women farmers?
questions.explanation: The Jharkhand government has introduced the ‘Mahila Khushhali Yojna’ for women farmers, as announced in its 2026-27 state budget presented on February 24, 2026. The scheme aims to empower women in agriculture by providing support for modern techniques and marketing platforms, with an initial allocation of ₹25 crore. The scheme focuses on enhancing the participation of women in the agricultural sector, offering improved access to modern techniques, and expanding cold storage-enabled cooperative marketing complexes.

questions.question: Which state government has recently re-implemented the Bhavantar Bhugtan Yojana (Price Difference Scheme) for the 2025-26 season?
questions.explanation: The Madhya Pradesh government, under Chief Minister Mohan Yadav, has recently re-implemented the Bhavantar Bhugtan Yojana (Price Difference Scheme) for the 2025-26 season to protect farmers from falling market prices. The government is extending this scheme to mustard and offering bonuses for urad, aiming to cover price deficits below the Minimum Support Price (MSP). The scheme was reintroduced in September 2025 for soybean and is being expanded in early 2026 to help farmers manage lower market rates

questions.question: The Eligible Manufacturer Importers (EMI) Scheme has been launched by which organization?

options.label: B
options.text: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs

options.label: D
options.text: Securities and Exchange Board of India

answer.label: B
answer.text: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs

questions.explanation: Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs has issued guidelines for the Eligible Manufacturer Importers (EMI) scheme. The Eligible Manufacturer Importers (EMI) Scheme has been launched by the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC). It is a trust-based facilitation measure for compliant manufacturers. EMI allows importers to clear goods without paying Customs duty at clearance. Duty can be paid monthly under Deferred Payment of Import Duty Rules, 2016. Scheme is valid from 1 April 2026 to 31 March 2028. It ensures faster clearance, priority treatment, ease of doing business, and promotes domestic manufacturing.

questions.question: The “Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Yatra Scheme” has been launched by which state/UT government?
questions.explanation: The first batch under the Dr B R Ambedkar Yatra Scheme of Puducherry Government started their journey to visit places linked with B. R. Ambedkar. The yatra was flagged off at Puducherry Railway Station by Lieutenant Governor K Kailashnathan. The “Dr. B.R. Ambedkar Yatra Scheme” has been launched by the Government of Puducherry. The scheme is implemented by the Adi Dravidar and Scheduled Tribes Welfare Department of Puducherry. The government is spending ₹40,000 per beneficiary for the yatra. The aim is to spread awareness about Dr Ambedkar’s life and legacy and improve the scheme through participant feedback.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government in March 2026 to encourage women entrepreneurship?

options.label: A
options.text: Tamil Nadu Women Entrepreneur Empowerment Scheme

answer.label: A
answer.text: Tamil Nadu Women Entrepreneur Empowerment Scheme

questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin launched the Tamil Nadu Women Entrepreneur Empowerment Scheme (TWEES) to support one lakh women in starting businesses over five years. The scheme is designed by the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) Department to promote women’s entrepreneurship across the State. The scheme aims to provide training, resources, and opportunities to encourage women-led enterprises.
